title: placeholder: combination-namespaces test taking 30m
body:
Placeholder, because this is going to take me a long time to isolate and I want eyes on it ASAP.

The `combination-namespaces` test is taking almost 30m in Rawhide. This is causing gating-test timeouts and failures. I can easily
reproduce on a 1minutetip VM:
```
# bats -T ...
...
 ✓ idmapping [131415]
 ✓ ipc-namespace [76657]
 ✓ net-namespace [61051]
 ✓ network-namespace [58675]
 ✓ pid-namespace [69845]
 ✓ user-namespace [35903]
 ✓ uts-namespace [72815]
 ✓ combination-namespaces [1764125]      <--- 1764s = 0:29:24
```
(All the other namespace tests feel like they take longer, too, but I don't have hard data. Just a gut feel).

`git log` shows no recent changes to the [bats file](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commits/main/tests/namespaces.bats). The last time it was touched, in November, has a namespace-related comment, but when I look at the diff I don't see how that could cause this huge delay.

comments.body: Closing. Several hours into my headbanging, tests started running much faster. (And, the sidetrack failure I was investigating, started passing too). I'm 80% convinced this was a CDN failure with the alpine APK mirrors; or at least part of it was manifesting that way. Anyhow, a [bodhi test rerun](https://osci-jenkins-1.ci.fedoraproject.org/job/fedora-ci/job/dist-git-pipeline/job/master/108095/testReport/(root)/tests/root_test/) almost passed, so I'm just going to attribute this to gremlins and move on.

Nope, reopening. Something really weird is going on. New test run on new VM, combination-namespaces took >35m to run, but it was already running super-slow before that. Total run was 115 minutes (compared to 99 for what I _thought_ was a slow run this morning). Timing results (poorly formatted, sorry):
